text:
I believe that there is more interest in Photonics again
$LITE $COHR $SIVE $AAOI $NOK etc.

So my goal leading into earnings is to give a quick pulse check on where my favorite companies are at

> What each business will look like over the next four quarters

> Which products are coming online and changing the revenue mix

> How quickly revenue and margins should grow

> What next-quarter guidance already implies

> How much execution is embedded in the current market cap

And so on

I haven't been posting too much on X lately but I will use this as a series to get a bit more active on here

Hopefully it will bring some value!

Either by seeing opportunity and building conviction

or 

By getting a clearer picture that you still think they are overvalued

Both could be valuable!

text:
🚨 Scientists Just Did Something That Feels Impossible

Quantum teleportation is real — but it does not move physical matter. Instead, scientists send the exact quantum information of a particle so it can be recreated somewhere else almost instantly.

The original disappears, while the new version appears far away, making it look like reality itself is being copied.

Experts believe this strange discovery could lead to futuristic communication systems and powerful quantum computers that may change the world forever.

text:
Dropped $100K into the market here into a fresh "I bought the dip" account. 

Risk reward looks fantastic to just go long a big list of my favorite stocks, some down 30-50%. I see no reason to concentrate this capital when there's this many deals across themes. 

Indexes are holding up well, the AI buildout is accelerating, earnings are coming up, and economic data is relatively strong. This looks like a generational retail shakeout, where leverage is just getting unwound, and sentiment is driving not facts and data.

I feel like we might be towards the back end of this high beta rinse. But of course my timing could be wrong. Things could fall further on Iran escalation which of course has a knock on effect with interest rates, which could certainly mean major pain ahead. 

Adding to each of my 6 @joinautopilot portfolios equally and then to my favorite international stocks, other picks, and microcaps not reflected there.

text:
*Monolithic (why does it auto correct everything🔫)

Photonics is simply transmitting data w/ light instead of electricity over copper, which wastes ~80% of its power & succumbs to Dennard scaling.

And it’s photonics that allows for computer processing to go modular w/ chiplets. https://t.co/LCMONL0jB1

PORTFOLIO UPDATE 7/15
$FROG   $AVGO   $LLY   $HIMS   $TWST   $SNOW   $CRWD

Hey guys — bifurcated tape today. April leaders in memory, AI, opticals, photonics all under distribution, trading below declining 21dma-structure. Other side of the market, leading groups and themes still working.

Market picture:
Nasdaq weak while SPX and Russell holding up. QQQE below 21dma-structure and MCSI hook-down — the tech/semis/AI trade is under distribution. Using /ES as the leading index here.

LLs still holding structure. RSP/SPX still holding structure too. VIX solid downside reversal off declining 21dma-structure rejection. Same on Credit Spreads.

Dell and AMD — last standing in growth. Shook out hard today, held their 21dma, bought decently.

So all in all: using themes to stay positioned in the right groups. Testing ONE in tech with AVGO — MC getting inflow, good RS. AAPL new high, AMZN/MSFT popping off structure. Added FROG today too.

On my book:
Fresh layer is on but not getting paid yet. NE Δ negative — new stuff didn't give me the traction I want. Book is top heavy on new, NE/CE 2.97x, cushion isn't the biggest.

NER and delta were pushing too high pre-close. Closed TEM and DT to bring both down and stay light side of margin overnight. Not a market call — positions showed RS today and feedback is good.

Good luck guys ✌️

Today's action:
NEW: AVGO, FROG
ADDED:
OUT: TEM, DT

text:
Wieczorne podsumowanie rynków | 15.07.2026 📈📉

Środa przyniosła rynkom mieszany obraz. Z jednej strony mieliśmy kolejne dobre dane inflacyjne z USA, bo po niższym CPI przyszedł słabszy od oczekiwań PPI. Z drugiej strony GPW złapała lekką zadyszkę po rekordowym rajdzie, a sektor półprzewodników znów pokazał, że AI trade wchodzi w coraz bardziej selektywną fazę 🤖⚖️.

Na warszawskim parkiecie rekordy nadal się pojawiały, ale końcówka dnia należała już do podaży. WIG20 poprawił intradayowy szczyt hossy, WIG również ustanowił nowy rekord w trakcie sesji, a mWIG40 zdobył historyczne maksimum. Mimo tego większość indeksów zakończyła dzień pod kreską 🇵🇱📉.

🇵🇱 GPW: Rekordy w trakcie dnia, spadki na zamknięciu 🦅
WIG20 zakończył sesję spadkiem o 0,41%, choć wcześniej poprawił szczyt obecnej hossy, dochodząc do 3 803,37 pkt. Szeroki WIG również ustanowił nowy rekord intraday na poziomie 144 074,70 pkt, ale finalnie stracił 0,26%.

Najlepiej wyglądał mWIG40, który wzrósł o 0,25% i ustanowił nowy historyczny rekord na poziomie 10 034,26 pkt. Słabiej wypadły małe spółki - sWIG80 spadł o 0,66%.

Obroty były bardzo solidne i wyniosły 2,83 mld zł, z czego 2,34 mld zł przypadło na WIG20. To pokazuje, że po rekordowym wtorku duży kapitał nadal aktywnie handlował największymi spółkami 💰.

Banki nadal pod presją: Głównym problemem GPW pozostał sektor finansowy. Rynek wciąż trawi pomysł podniesienia CIT dla banków, co mogłoby uderzyć w zyski sektora. WIG-Banki stracił 1,39%, a pod kreską znalazły się https://t.co/OCLtOl4R0R. mBank (-2,21%), Erste (-2,15%), PKO BP (-1,27%), Alior (-1,20%) i Pekao (-0,63%) 🏦📉.

Modivo najsłabsze w WIG20: Największy spadek wśród blue chipów zaliczyło Modivo (-4,13%). Po mocniejszych wcześniejszych ruchach inwestorzy zdecydowali się na realizację zysków. Indeksowi ciążył też KGHM (-1,88%), który mimo wysokiej zmienności na metalach nadal nie potrafi złapać stabilnego trendu wzrostowego ⛏️.

Orlen lekko pod kreską: Paliwowy gigant spadł o 0,34%, mimo że ropa pozostaje wysoko z powodu napięć wokół Iranu i Cieśniny Ormuz. Rynek najwyraźniej uznał, że po ostatnim mocnym rajdzie spółka potrzebuje chwili oddechu ⛽.

Allegro próbuje bronić indeksu: Po zielonej stronie ponownie znalazło się Allegro (+1,30%), które kontynuuje dobrą passę po publikacji szacunków za II kwartał. Skorygowana EBITDA wzrosła o 11,5% rdr do 1,03 mld zł, a GMV w Polsce przyspieszyło o 12%. Po wtorkowym wystrzale popyt nadal nie odpuścił 🛒🔥.

Pepco i Dino na plusie: Dobrze wyglądało Pepco (+2,75%), a Dino wzrosło o 1,24%, próbując odbić po wcześniejszej słabości. To właśnie te spółki, obok Allegro, ograniczały skalę przeceny WIG20.

Średnie spółki mocniejsze: W mWIG40 liderem był Polimex Mostostal (+7,00%), mocno wzrósł też Mirbud (+4,58%). JSW zakończyło dzień lekko nad kreską po publikacji danych operacyjnych za II kwartał, z produkcją węgla na poziomie 3,23 mln ton.

Małe spółki z dużą zmiennością: W sWIG80 mocno spadło VIGO Photonics (-7,41%) po informacji o ABB na nowe i istniejące akcje. Wyraźnie traciły też Creotech Quantum (-6,90%) i Scanway (-4,75%). Pozytywnie wyróżnił się Zremb-Chojnice (+7,56%) oraz Onde (+2,16%) po kontrakcie na budowę magazynu energii dla grupy R.Power. Spektakularny rajd kontynuowała Krakchemia (+25,44%) 🚀.

🇺🇸 Wall Street: PPI pomaga, ale chipy znów hamują Nasdaq 🇺🇸
Za oceanem nastroje były lepsze niż na GPW. W trakcie sesji Dow Jones, S&P 500 i Nasdaq znajdowały się lekko nad kreską. Pomagały przede wszystkim dane o cenach producentów. PPI w USA spadł w czerwcu o 0,3% m/m, choć rynek oczekiwał wyniku bliskiego zera. To kolejny, po wtorkowym CPI, sygnał, że presja inflacyjna chwilowo słabnie 🧊.

Nie oznacza to jednak pełnego komfortu dla Fedu. Czerwcowe dane nie pokazują jeszcze w pełni efektu ostatniego skoku cen ropy, a Lisa Cook z Fed zasugerowała, że jeśli inflacja nie będzie dalej hamować, bank centralny pozostaje gotowy do działania 🏦.

Wyniki finansów wspierają rynek: Sezon wyników w USA zaczął się solidnie. BlackRock przebił oczekiwania i mocno rósł, a Morgan Stanley pokazał lepszy od prognoz zysk, choć kurs reagował spokojniej. Sektor finansowy w USA wygląda dużo lepiej niż banki na GPW, gdzie tematem numer jeden jest lokalne ryzyko podatkowe 📊.

PayPal z mocnym ruchem: Jednym z najgorętszych walorów dnia był $PYPL, który mocno zyskiwał po doniesieniach o ofercie przejęcia ze strony Stripe i Advent International. Rynek natychmiast zaczął wyceniać potencjalną premię transakcyjną 💳.

ASML pokazuje siłę AI infrastruktury: ASML podniosło prognozy sprzedaży i zapowiedziało zwiększenie mocy produkcyjnych sprzętu dla producentów chipów o 30%. To teoretycznie bardzo dobry sygnał dla całego łańcucha AI, bo popyt na infrastrukturę pozostaje silny.

Problem w tym, że nie wszystkie chipy korzystają tak samo. Micron i SK Hynix mocno spadały, a indeks półprzewodników był pod presją. Rynek coraz wyraźniej rozróżnia zwycięzców AI od spółek, które mają już bardzo dużo optymizmu w cenie 💻📉.

🌍 Europa: Bez jednego kierunku 🇪🇺
Na Starym Kontynencie nastroje były mieszane. DAX tracił ok. 0,5%, FTSE 100 krążył blisko poziomów odniesienia, a CAC 40 lekko zyskiwał.

Europa nie dostała tak mocnego impulsu jak Wall Street, mimo dobrego PPI z USA. Inwestorzy nadal patrzą na ropę, Iran i ryzyko inflacyjne. Po ostatnich wzrostach część rynku po prostu wybrała ostrożność zamiast dalszego gonienia indeksów 🌍.

💱 Waluty, obligacje i Fed: Dane pomagają, ropa przeszkadza 💵
Niższy PPI z USA poprawił nastroje na długu i ograniczył presję na szybkie podwyżki stóp. Rynek zaczął wyceniać dużo mniejsze prawdopodobieństwo ruchu Fed już na najbliższym posiedzeniu.

Problem w tym, że Fed nadal nie może ogłosić zwycięstwa nad inflacją. Ceny ropy wzrosły w pierwszej połowie lipca, a konflikt USA-Iran pozostaje ryzykiem dla kosztów energii, transportu i oczekiwań inflacyjnych.

Dolar był nieco słabszy po danych, ale bez dużej paniki. Złoty pozostawał relatywnie stabilny, choć lokalnie ciąży mu perspektywa ewentualnych obniżek stóp po wakacjach oraz presja na sektor bankowy.

🛢️ Ropa: Hormuz wciąż trzyma rynek w napięciu ⛽
Ropa pozostaje jednym z kluczowych czynników ryzyka. Trump zapowiedział intensyfikację ataków na Iran, jeśli Teheran nie usiądzie do rozmów i nie odblokuje Cieśniny Ormuz. To utrzymuje geopolityczną premię w cenach surowca ⚠️.

Nawet jeśli dzisiejsze dane PPI wyglądają dobrze, rynek wie, że kolejny szok energetyczny mógłby szybko zmienić obraz inflacji. Dlatego ropa nadal jest jednym z głównych elementów układanki dla Fedu, Wall Street i rynków wschodzących.

💡 Wniosek dnia:
Środa była dniem schłodzenia po mocnym rajdzie. GPW znów poprawiała rekordy w trakcie sesji, ale finalnie zabrakło paliwa do utrzymania wzrostów. Największym problemem pozostają banki, które znalazły się pod presją politycznego ryzyka podatkowego. Do tego doszły spadki Modivo, KGHM i słabszy Orlen.

Jednocześnie nie można mówić o załamaniu rynku. Allegro, Pepco, Dino i część średnich spółek nadal przyciągały kapitał, a mWIG40 ustanowił nowy historyczny szczyt. To raczej selektywna realizacja zysków niż paniczna ucieczka.

Na świecie inwestorzy dostali drugi z rzędu dobry sygnał inflacyjny z USA, tym razem w postaci niższego PPI. Wall Street przyjęła to pozytywnie, ale słabość półprzewodników pokazuje, że AI trade nie jest już prostą historią „kupuj wszystko”. ASML pokazało siłę popytu, ale Micron i SK Hynix przypomniały, że wyceny i oczekiwania też mają znaczenie.

Najważniejszy temat pozostaje ten sam: czy niższe dane inflacyjne wystarczą, żeby uspokoić Fed, jeśli ropa utrzyma się wysoko przez konflikt wokół Hormuzu? Dzisiejsza sesja dała rynkom oddech, ale nie zamknęła żadnego z głównych ryzyk.

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 6135}, {"from_index": 6605, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 6618}]}, "text": "Wieczorne podsumowanie rynków | 15.07.2026 📈📉 Środa przyniosła rynkom mieszany obraz. Z jednej strony mieliśmy kolejne dobre dane inflacyjne z USA, bo po niższym CPI przyszedł słabszy od oczekiwań PPI. Z drugiej strony GPW złapała lekką zadyszkę po rekordowym rajdzie, a sektor półprzewodników znów pokazał, że AI trade wchodzi w coraz bardziej selektywną fazę 🤖⚖️. Na warszawskim parkiecie rekordy nadal się pojawiały, ale końcówka dnia należała już do podaży. WIG20 poprawił intradayowy szczyt hossy, WIG również ustanowił nowy rekord w trakcie sesji, a mWIG40 zdobył historyczne maksimum. Mimo tego większość indeksów zakończyła dzień pod kreską 🇵🇱📉. 🇵🇱 GPW: Rekordy w trakcie dnia, spadki na zamknięciu 🦅 WIG20 zakończył sesję spadkiem o 0,41%, choć wcześniej poprawił szczyt obecnej hossy, dochodząc do 3 803,37 pkt. Szeroki WIG również ustanowił nowy rekord intraday na poziomie 144 074,70 pkt, ale finalnie stracił 0,26%. Najlepiej wyglądał mWIG40, który wzrósł o 0,25% i ustanowił nowy historyczny rekord na poziomie 10 034,26 pkt. Słabiej wypadły małe spółki - sWIG80 spadł o 0,66%. Obroty były bardzo solidne i wyniosły 2,83 mld zł, z czego 2,34 mld zł przypadło na WIG20. To pokazuje, że po rekordowym wtorku duży kapitał nadal aktywnie handlował największymi spółkami 💰. Banki nadal pod presją: Głównym problemem GPW pozostał sektor finansowy. Rynek wciąż trawi pomysł podniesienia CIT dla banków, co mogłoby uderzyć w zyski sektora. WIG-Banki stracił 1,39%, a pod kreską znalazły się https://t.co/OCLtOl4R0R. mBank (-2,21%), Erste (-2,15%), PKO BP (-1,27%), Alior (-1,20%) i Pekao (-0,63%) 🏦📉. Modivo najsłabsze w WIG20: Największy spadek wśród blue chipów zaliczyło Modivo (-4,13%). Po mocniejszych wcześniejszych ruchach inwestorzy zdecydowali się na realizację zysków. Indeksowi ciążył też KGHM (-1,88%), który mimo wysokiej zmienności na metalach nadal nie potrafi złapać stabilnego trendu wzrostowego ⛏️. Orlen lekko pod kreską: Paliwowy gigant spadł o 0,34%, mimo że ropa pozostaje wysoko z powodu napięć wokół Iranu i Cieśniny Ormuz. Rynek najwyraźniej uznał, że po ostatnim mocnym rajdzie spółka potrzebuje chwili oddechu ⛽. Allegro próbuje bronić indeksu: Po zielonej stronie ponownie znalazło się Allegro (+1,30%), które kontynuuje dobrą passę po publikacji szacunków za II kwartał. Skorygowana EBITDA wzrosła o 11,5% rdr do 1,03 mld zł, a GMV w Polsce przyspieszyło o 12%. Po wtorkowym wystrzale popyt nadal nie odpuścił 🛒🔥. Pepco i Dino na plusie: Dobrze wyglądało Pepco (+2,75%), a Dino wzrosło o 1,24%, próbując odbić po wcześniejszej słabości. To właśnie te spółki, obok Allegro, ograniczały skalę przeceny WIG20. Średnie spółki mocniejsze: W mWIG40 liderem był Polimex Mostostal (+7,00%), mocno wzrósł też Mirbud (+4,58%). JSW zakończyło dzień lekko nad kreską po publikacji danych operacyjnych za II kwartał, z produkcją węgla na poziomie 3,23 mln ton. Małe spółki z dużą zmiennością: W sWIG80 mocno spadło VIGO Photonics (-7,41%) po informacji o ABB na nowe i istniejące akcje. Wyraźnie traciły też Creotech Quantum (-6,90%) i Scanway (-4,75%). Pozytywnie wyróżnił się Zremb-Chojnice (+7,56%) oraz Onde (+2,16%) po kontrakcie na budowę magazynu energii dla grupy R.Power. Spektakularny rajd kontynuowała Krakchemia (+25,44%) 🚀. 🇺🇸 Wall Street: PPI pomaga, ale chipy znów hamują Nasdaq 🇺🇸 Za oceanem nastroje były lepsze niż na GPW. W trakcie sesji Dow Jones, S&P 500 i Nasdaq znajdowały się lekko nad kreską. Pomagały przede wszystkim dane o cenach producentów. PPI w USA spadł w czerwcu o 0,3% m/m, choć rynek oczekiwał wyniku bliskiego zera. To kolejny, po wtorkowym CPI, sygnał, że presja inflacyjna chwilowo słabnie 🧊. Nie oznacza to jednak pełnego komfortu dla Fedu. Czerwcowe dane nie pokazują jeszcze w pełni efektu ostatniego skoku cen ropy, a Lisa Cook z Fed zasugerowała, że jeśli inflacja nie będzie dalej hamować, bank centralny pozostaje gotowy do działania 🏦. Wyniki finansów wspierają rynek: Sezon wyników w USA zaczął się solidnie. BlackRock przebił oczekiwania i mocno rósł, a Morgan Stanley pokazał lepszy od prognoz zysk, choć kurs reagował spokojniej. Sektor finansowy w USA wygląda dużo lepiej niż banki na GPW, gdzie tematem numer jeden jest lokalne ryzyko podatkowe 📊. PayPal z mocnym ruchem: Jednym z najgorętszych walorów dnia był $PYPL, który mocno zyskiwał po doniesieniach o ofercie przejęcia ze strony Stripe i Advent International. Rynek natychmiast zaczął wyceniać potencjalną premię transakcyjną 💳. ASML pokazuje siłę AI infrastruktury: ASML podniosło prognozy sprzedaży i zapowiedziało zwiększenie mocy produkcyjnych sprzętu dla producentów chipów o 30%. To teoretycznie bardzo dobry sygnał dla całego łańcucha AI, bo popyt na infrastrukturę pozostaje silny. Problem w tym, że nie wszystkie chipy korzystają tak samo. Micron i SK Hynix mocno spadały, a indeks półprzewodników był pod presją. Rynek coraz wyraźniej rozróżnia zwycięzców AI od spółek, które mają już bardzo dużo optymizmu w cenie 💻📉. 🌍 Europa: Bez jednego kierunku 🇪🇺 Na Starym Kontynencie nastroje były mieszane. DAX tracił ok. 0,5%, FTSE 100 krążył blisko poziomów odniesienia, a CAC 40 lekko zyskiwał. Europa nie dostała tak mocnego impulsu jak Wall Street, mimo dobrego PPI z USA. Inwestorzy nadal patrzą na ropę, Iran i ryzyko inflacyjne. Po ostatnich wzrostach część rynku po prostu wybrała ostrożność zamiast dalszego gonienia indeksów 🌍. 💱 Waluty, obligacje i Fed: Dane pomagają, ropa przeszkadza 💵 Niższy PPI z USA poprawił nastroje na długu i ograniczył presję na szybkie podwyżki stóp. Rynek zaczął wyceniać dużo mniejsze prawdopodobieństwo ruchu Fed już na najbliższym posiedzeniu. Problem w tym, że Fed nadal nie może ogłosić zwycięstwa nad inflacją. Ceny ropy wzrosły w pierwszej połowie lipca, a konflikt USA-Iran pozostaje ryzykiem dla kosztów energii, transportu i oczekiwań inflacyjnych. Dolar był nieco słabszy po danych, ale bez dużej paniki. Złoty pozostawał relatywnie stabilny, choć lokalnie ciąży mu perspektywa ewentualnych obniżek stóp po wakacjach oraz presja na sektor bankowy. 🛢️ Ropa: Hormuz wciąż trzyma rynek w napięciu ⛽ Ropa pozostaje jednym z kluczowych czynników ryzyka. Trump zapowiedział intensyfikację ataków na Iran, jeśli Teheran nie usiądzie do rozmów i nie odblokuje Cieśniny Ormuz. To utrzymuje geopolityczną premię w cenach surowca ⚠️. Nawet jeśli dzisiejsze dane PPI wyglądają dobrze, rynek wie, że kolejny szok energetyczny mógłby szybko zmienić obraz inflacji. Dlatego ropa nadal jest jednym z głównych elementów układanki dla Fedu, Wall Street i rynków wschodzących. 💡 Wniosek dnia: Środa była dniem schłodzenia po mocnym rajdzie. GPW znów poprawiała rekordy w trakcie sesji, ale finalnie zabrakło paliwa do utrzymania wzrostów. Największym problemem pozostają banki, które znalazły się pod presją politycznego ryzyka podatkowego. Do tego doszły spadki Modivo, KGHM i słabszy Orlen. Jednocześnie nie można mówić o załamaniu rynku. Allegro, Pepco, Dino i część średnich spółek nadal przyciągały kapitał, a mWIG40 ustanowił nowy historyczny szczyt. To raczej selektywna realizacja zysków niż paniczna ucieczka. Na świecie inwestorzy dostali drugi z rzędu dobry sygnał inflacyjny z USA, tym razem w postaci niższego PPI. Wall Street przyjęła to pozytywnie, ale słabość półprzewodników pokazuje, że AI trade nie jest już prostą historią „kupuj wszystko”. ASML pokazało siłę popytu, ale Micron i SK Hynix przypomniały, że wyceny i oczekiwania też mają znaczenie. Najważniejszy temat pozostaje ten sam: czy niższe dane inflacyjne wystarczą, żeby uspokoić Fed, jeśli ropa utrzyma się wysoko przez konflikt wokół Hormuzu? Dzisiejsza sesja dała rynkom oddech, ale nie zamknęła żadnego z głównych ryzyk. #Giełda #GPW #WallStreet #Nasdaq #Fed #ASML #Micron #AI #Iran #FinTwitt #Inwestowanie"}}}, "rest_id": "2077479870192373912", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "725", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

morgan stanley dropped a CPO note today and the laser supply chain is a lot more concentrated than most people realize

the three western names that actually matter here: $SIVE, COHR, LITE

sitting next to switch platforms from broadcom and nvidia. optical engines from lightmatter, ayar labs, marvell/celestial, poet. silicon photonics foundry capacity at TSM, GFS, TSEM

rosenblatt put out a separate note saying chinese laser suppliers are nowhere close on CPO-grade lasers. that matters

so you've got a $1.5B company in SIVE sitting next to two $60B+ giants as one of the critical global players. morgan stanley basically validated what a few of us have been saying about the laser chokepoint

not many ways to play CPO at the component level. this is one of them

text:
$AEHR deep dive:

Financials are ugly backward-looking, but interesting forward-looking. TTM revenue was USD 45.26M, TTM gross margin was 30.72%, free cash flow was negative USD 12.19M, and FY2027 consensus revenue was USD 85.14M, or about 71% growth.

The thesis is that Aehr is becoming a tollbooth for testing the fragile stuff inside AI hardware: high-power AI ASICs, advanced packages, silicon photonics, and optical I/O. If these chips fail inside a data-center system, the customer has a very expensive bad day.

The catalysts are real: Q3 FY2026 bookings were USD 37.2M, effective backlog was USD 50.9M, then Aehr announced a record USD 41M hyperscale AI package-level burn-in order. On June 17, it added a follow-on FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in system order from a major silicon-photonics customer, configured for nine wafers in parallel and scheduled for delivery within six months.

The catch: the new SiPh order value and customer name were not disclosed, and the customer forecast for more systems this year is not backlog until purchase orders show up. At roughly USD 112, the stock was already around USD 3B-plus market cap, with stale analyst targets around USD 64. That is not exactly a bargain bin.

Risks: valuation, customer concentration, bookings-to-revenue timing, gross margin recovery, and the classic small-cap semi equipment problem where everyone falls in love with the order headline before the revenue and margin proof arrives.

Entry/Verdict: HOLD / catalyst watch / no add-size chase. Tiny tracker only if you want event exposure. Real add-size waits for FY2027 guidance above USD 100M-125M minimum, ideally toward USD 140M+, plus backlog conversion, margin recovery, and estimate revisions.

The tech signal is real. The stock just needs the numbers to stop being theoretical. We'll see how it goes.

text:
Morgan Stanley’s CPO report supports one of the more important AI infrastructure ideas:

The next bottleneck may not be compute.

It may be optical connectivity.

Key names across the CPO stack:

$SIVE $COHR $LITE — lasers
$AVGO $NVDA — switch platforms
Lightmatter / Ayar Labs / $MRVL / POET — photonics
$TSM $GFS $TSEM — silicon photonics foundry capacity
The company I keep coming back to is $SIVE.
A ~$1.5B laser supplier positioned inside a strategic bottleneck where Western leadership may matter more as CPO adoption scales.

text:
The CPO supply chain is starting to look like a serious AI infrastructure theme.

Morgan Stanley’s latest report highlights:

• Laser supply chain: $SIVE $COHR $LITE
• Switch platforms: $AVGO $NVDA
• Optical engines: Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $MRVL, Celestial, POET
• Silicon photonics foundries: $TSM $GFS $TSEM
My focus remains on $SIVE.
Why?
Because CPO needs high-performance laser suppliers, and Rosenblatt’s view suggests Chinese competitors are still far from meeting those requirements.

That makes Western laser companies much more important than the market may realize.

text:
AI infrastructure is not just GPUs.

CPO could become one of the most important bottlenecks in next-generation AI networking.

Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report points to several key players:

$SIVE $COHR $LITE — laser supply chain
$AVGO $NVDA — switch platforms
Lightmatter / Ayar Labs / $MRVL / POET — optical engines
$TSM $GFS $TSEM — silicon photonics capacity
The standout is $SIVE.
At around $1.5B, it is small compared with the scale of the AI infrastructure buildout, but its laser role may become increasingly critical.

text:
Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report lines up closely with the research I’ve been tracking.

Key CPO beneficiaries appear across the stack:

🔹 Lasers: $SIVE $COHR $LITE
🔹 Switch platforms: $AVGO $NVDA
🔹 Optical engines / photonics: Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $MRVL, Celestial, POET
🔹 Silicon photonics foundry: $TSM $GFS $TSEM
The name that stands out most to me is $SIVE.
At roughly a $1.5B market cap, SIVE is becoming increasingly important in the laser supply chain for CPO.
If Chinese laser suppliers remain behind the required CPO performance curve, Western laser leaders could become a major strategic bottleneck.

text:
Wavelength-scale nonlocal metadevices shatter finite-size Q limits, trapping light like an infinite crystal in a ring the size of a single wavelength while emitting pristine vector beams on demand. Researchers from Harbin Institute of Technology (Shenzhen), Pengcheng Laboratory, and collaborators fused bound states in the continuum (BICs) with topology to engineer nonlocal metasurfaces bent into closed, continuous-boundary rings. 

Traditional finite nonlocal metasurfaces bleed radiation at edges, capping quality factors. Seamless loops eliminate lateral radiative channels, yielding experimental Q factors approaching 10^5 at true wavelength scales and rivaling ideal 1D infinite quasi-BICs. This topological approach conquers the boundary curse. 

Strong meta-atom collective interactions suppress unwanted radiation while preserving controllable vertical emission. Multipole analysis shows the BIC mode dominated by magnetic dipole and electric quadrupole contributions, minimizing leakage unlike the leaky mode’s toroidal and quadrupole-heavy profile. Size-dependent tests confirm saturation at large radii matches the infinite-case limit, with robust fabrication tolerances against grating shifts, overlays, and height variations.

Dual vector beam generation, radial and azimuthal polarizations, plus OAM beams and nonlinear responses from one compact device. On-chip photonics gains high-Q, mode-multiplexing capability for ultra-efficient light trapping in sensors, lasers, quantum tech, and structured-light applications, all in smaller-than-conventional footprints.

Photonic chips can now confine light in whispering-gallery-like modes while emitting engineered vortices on cue, bridging classical confinement with nonlocal coherence. Like bending a leaky fence into an unbreakable Möbius loop, this seamless marriage of metasurface nonlocality and microcavity topology marks a record advance. Points toward scalable integrated photonics that defy size-Q trade-offs.

text:
Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report validates my research:

Key CPO players include:

🔹 Laser supply chain: $SIVE, $COHR, $LITE

🔹 Switch platforms: $Broadcom, $Nvidia

🔹 Optical engines & photonics:
$Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $Marvell/Celestial, POET

🔹 Silicon photonics foundry capacity: $TSM, $GFS, $TSEM

What stands out is $SIVE — a ~$1.5B laser company that is emerging as a critical player in the CPO ecosystem.
Combined with Rosenblatt’s view that Chinese laser suppliers are still far from meeting CPO requirements, the importance of the leading Western laser companies in this strategic bottleneck becomes even clearer.

@aleabitoreddit Photonics race

CPO could become a major tech battleground ahead

$IQE

The ultimate AI photonics turnaround story is happening right now: $IQE (IQE plc) is quietly positioning itself at the epicentre of the global hardware super-cycle.

While retail investors obsess over overhyped silicon plays, this UK semiconductor giant has spent the year completely transforming its business. By providing the advanced compound semiconductor and epitaxial wafers that allow chips to transmit data via light rather than electricity, IQE is resolving the biggest physical speed bottlenecks in AI data centres.

Why the macro bull case is exceptionally strong right now:

🔹 The MACOM Endorsement: Secured a transformational £81 million strategic investment and long-term supply agreement with Nasdaq-listed titan ⁠MACOM Technology Solutions, completely wiping out previous balance sheet worries.

🔹 Explosive Revenue Guidance: Management is officially tracking toward >20% year-on-year revenue growth, backed by a massive influx of orders for their Indium Phosphide (InP) photonics solutions.

🔹 High-Margin Operating Leverage: As factory capacity utilization scales back up across the UK, US, and Taiwan, their adjusted EBITDA is projected to catapult into high-single to low-double digits.

🔹 Huge Analyst Price Targets: Major City institutions (like Deutsche Numis) have strongly backed the turnaround, initiating a 60p price target—representing a massive upside from its current consolidation range.

The massive inventory correction that plagued the industry is over, and the AI data centre boom has officially arrived. Don't miss the boat on this structural rebound.

text:
$SIVE

Don't let the recent pullback fool you—the smart money is aggressively buying the dip on $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors).

While the broader market fixated on temporary dilution, the Sivers Board of Directors and CEO just dropped a massive vote of confidence by buying a wave of shares directly on the open market. Sivers isn't just riding the AI photonics wave; they are providing the foundational laser technology powering it.

Why the structural bull case is stronger than ever:

🔹 The Hyperscaler Pipeline: Supplying customized, high-performance laser arrays that replace slow copper wiring with high-speed light inside major AI data clusters.

🔹 Massive Operating Leverage: Its sales pipeline expanded exponentially to nearly $800 million, setting up explosive multi-year growth as Tier-1 partnerships scale.

🔹 U.S. Dual-Listing Catalyst: Sivers is actively pushing toward a U.S. Nasdaq dual-listing, which will open the floodgates to massive pools of Wall Street institutional capital.

🔹 Extreme Short Squeeze Potential: With heavy short interest betting against it during this temporary correction, any major contract announcement risks triggering an epic, forced buyback rally.

Insiders are locking up shares because they know what's coming next. This is a prime optical infrastructure player sitting on a generational tech breakthrough.

text:
I mapped the entire future economy. Nine layers, from bedrock to orbit → and every layer is investable.

🛰️ SPACE → the orbital economy: $ASTS $RKLB $PL
⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE → powering the buildout: $TE $EOSE $SPKL
🚁 AUTONOMY & DRONES → machines that operate themselves: $ONDS $AUR $MRLN $KRKNF
🤖 PHYSICAL AI → intelligence that acts: $OUST $AMBA $OSS
💡 PHOTONICS → light replaces copper: $CRDO $SIVEF $AAOI $LITE 
🏗️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE → the compute buildout: $IREN $NBIS $DGXX $WYFI 
🔬 GLASS & OPTICAL MATERIALS → the substrate light runs on: $GLW $LPTH $LPKF
📡 CONNECTIVITY & RF → moving the signal: $AMPG
⛏️ CRITICAL MINERALS → the hard constraints under everything: $MP $UAMY $ASPI

Here's what the map teaches that a watchlist can't: these aren't nine separate trades. They're one machine → the minerals feed the glass, the glass carries the light, the light feeds the compute, the compute powers the intelligence, the intelligence drives the machines, and the machines reach for orbit. Every layer is a chokepoint for the one above it.

Which is the entire investing philosophy in one image: don't chase the logo at the top of the stack → own the layers everything above is FORCED to buy from. Logos compete. Layers collect.

Important note: a map is a thesis, not a timing tool → layers de-rate together when the factor gets sold (see: this month), and not every name survives to see its layer win. Diversification across a stack is still one machine.

The future economy isn't a sector. It's a supply chain → and it's hiring.

They're one machine → the minerals feed the glass, the glass carries the light, the light feeds the compute, the compute powers the intelligence, the intelligence drives the machines, and the machines reach for orbit. Every layer is a chokepoint for the one above it. Which is the entire investing philosophy in one image: don't chase the logo at the top of the stack → own the layers everything above is FORCED to buy from. Logos compete. Layers collect. Important note: a map is a thesis, not a timing tool → layers de-rate together when the factor gets sold (see: this month), and not every name survives to see its layer win. Diversification across a stack is still one machine. The future economy isn't a sector. text:
GPUs are the engine powering AI’s intelligence. Without efficient, high-bandwidth compute, the whole system hits a wall. This is like a brain without the body to act on its thoughts.
That’s why I’m invested in $NVDA, $NBIS, $IREN, $NOK and $SIVE.
$NBIS and $IREN provide the GPU cloud infrastructure at scale.
$NBIS is already putting AI brains into physical machines through its robotics efforts. Autonomous delivery robots and robotaxis are running in the real world.
$SIVE is developing external light source modules and photonics solutions specifically for AI data centers. This speeds up data movement dramatically inside the AI brain, reducing bottlenecks as models and clusters grow.
$NOK’s connectivity layer, with longer-term potential in advanced networks, will help distribute this intelligence across systems and into the next wave of advanced robots.
Anything that makes the AI engine faster, more efficient, or better connected is worth backing. The market will continue re-rating those closing these critical gaps. We are not early. The market is late in understanding the significance of it.

text:
You do realize China can implement more stringent export bans 

Rare-earth magnets (NdPr + Dy/Tb) → Cooling motors, pumps, chillers, generators and factory equipment; 6–18 months, potentially 2–3 years for full replacement capacity.

Gallium → GaN power supplies, RF chips and optical components; 3–12 months initially, 12–24 months for affected designs to qualify alternatives.

Germanium → Fiber-optic networks, photodetectors and silicon photonics; 3–9 months, longer for specialized high-speed networking.

Indium → Optical transceivers and lasers connecting GPU clusters; 3–9 months and higher networking costs.

Tungsten → Semiconductor-fab tooling, chip interconnect processes and data-center construction equipment; 3–9 months.

Antimony → Flame-retardant electronics, power equipment and backup batteries; 2–8 months.

Processed graphite → UPS batteries and grid-scale backup storage; 6–18 months, but GPUs themselves keep shipping.

Yttrium and specialized heavy rare earths → Chipmaking ceramics, coatings, lasers and high-temperature components; 6–18 months.

Fluorspar-derived chemicals → Semiconductor etching and cleaning chemicals; 3–12 months, although non-Chinese supply exists.

All restricted simultaneously → Existing GPU inventory still runs, but new AI data-center completion could fall sharply after 3–6 months and remain constrained for 18–36+ months.

text:
🚀 2026 Call for Applications: Postdoctoral Researcher Positions

We are pleased to announce the 2026 Call for Applications for postdoctoral researchers to join the Intelligent Defense Systems Research Group (IDS RG) through the InnoCORE Program at UNIST.
Following our successful first recruitment round, we are now launching a new call with updated compensation and expanded opportunities.

✅ Research Areas

We are seeking outstanding researchers in:
- RF Systems (Antennas, RF IC/Devices, Radar)
- Optics Systems (Photonics, Sensors, Optical Devices)
- Power Systems (Power Electronics, Control, GaN)
- Semiconductors (AI System-on-Chip, Electronic Devices)
- Network Systems (Agentic AI, Wireless Communications) 

💰 Compensation & Benefits

- Starting annual salary: KRW 90 million + α
  (approximately USD 65,000 + α, before taxes and fees)
- Higher compensation may be offered based on research achievements and experience
- Opportunities to attend leading international conferences
- Possibility of secondment to partner universities for collaborative research 

🌍 Eligibility

Applicants of all nationalities are welcome.
Candidates who are expected to complete their Ph.D. before the start date are also encouraged to apply. 

📅 Recruitment Timeline

- Application Deadline: July 31, 2026
- Interview Period: August 5–10, 2026
- Decision Notification: August 14, 2026
- Anticipated Start Date: September 1, 2026
  (The start date may be adjusted by mutual agreement.) 

📩 Apply

https://t.co/UEjn6pEXHT

🔗 Learn More

- ATL (Byun’s Group): https://t.co/fhuMgsLIU9
- IDS RG: https://t.co/FuJ0PVFI2c
- InnoCORE Program: https://t.co/Tm2KSyUied
- Nature Careers: https://t.co/yIfSeAZrdN
Please feel free to share this opportunity with outstanding candidates who may be interested.

@ IEEE Antennas and Propagation Society @IEEE AP-S Young Professionals
#Postdoc #ResearchJobs #UNIST #InnoCORE #Antenna #RF #Radar #Photonics #Semiconductor #WirelessCommunications #IEEEAPS

text:
Read an interesting article about the NPO/CPO debate over the weekend. Could be something interesting for the future so wanted to bring this to your mind.

But what is NPO?

Near-Packaged Optics (NPO) is an intermediate step between today’s pluggable optics and future Co-Packaged Optics (CPO). Instead of integrating the optical engine directly with the switch ASIC like CPO, NPO places it immediately next to the ASIC on the same substrate. This shortens electrical paths, reduces power consumption and signal loss, while keeping the optical engine replaceable and easier to manufacture.

Why is it important?

NPO is increasingly viewed as a practical bridge toward CPO. It avoids today’s CPO manufacturing bottlenecks, lower yields and ecosystem lock-in while allowing hyperscalers to deploy higher-performance optical networking much sooner. The industry still sees CPO as the long-term destination, but NPO could become an important transition architecture over the next several years as AI clusters continue scaling.

Who could benefit?

NPO still requires a massive increase in optical content. Regardless of whether hyperscalers deploy pluggables, NPO or eventually CPO, all paths point to significantly higher demand for InP lasers, optical engines and silicon photonics. Optical suppliers such as Lumentum ($LITE ) and Coherent ($COHR ) remain among the key long-term beneficiaries, while networking leaders Arista ($ANET ) and Ciena ($CIEN) also benefit from the broader scale-up of AI networking.

Could be something interesting for the future so wanted to bring this to your mind. But what is NPO? Near-Packaged Optics (NPO) is an intermediate step between today’s pluggable optics and future Co-Packaged Optics (CPO). Instead of integrating the optical engine directly with the switch ASIC like CPO, NPO places it immediately next to the ASIC on the same substrate. This shortens electrical paths, reduces power consumption and signal loss, while keeping the optical engine replaceable and easier to manufacture. Why is it important? NPO is increasingly viewed as a practical bridge toward CPO. It avoids today’s CPO manufacturing bottlenecks, lower yields and ecosystem lock-in while allowing hyperscalers to deploy higher-performance optical networking much sooner. The industry still sees CPO as the long-term destination, but NPO could become an important transition architecture over the next several years as AI clusters continue scaling. Who could benefit? NPO still requires a massive increase in optical content. Regardless of whether hyperscalers deploy pluggables, NPO or eventually CPO, all paths point to significantly higher demand for InP lasers, optical engines and silicon photonics. Optical suppliers such as Lumentum ($LITE ) and Coherent ($COHR ) remain among the key long-term beneficiaries, while networking leaders Arista ($ANET ) and Ciena ($CIEN) also benefit from the broader scale-up of AI networking."}}}, "rest_id": "2078891813155287457", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2040", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$GFS & $SIVE: The AI photonics story started years ago.  
 
The relationship was not built overnight after recent AI photonics headlines. Years earlier, $GFS invested in MixComm, taking a minority stake and aligning its RF-SOI manufacturing expertise with advanced millimeter-wave technology. 
 
When $SIVE acquired MixComm in 2022 for ~$135M, $GFS converted its ownership into public shares instead of exiting completely. 
 
This shows a long-term ecosystem strategy  combining manufacturing capability, equity alignment, and next-generation semiconductor innovation. The AI data center boom is now creating demand for faster, more efficient optical connectivity. 
 
Charts tell the story before headlines do.

text:
A couple years ago, the stock market had no AI, no Memory supercycle, no Photonics, no Quantum… name it.

Wasn’t it more fun back then? Less hype, less bullshit narratives, more actual quality businesses?

Or are we just in the middle of the biggest tech cycle of our lifetime? Curious to hear your take.

text:
Silicon photonics can win while half of the “silicon photonics stocks” still lose.

A growing TAM is not enough.
The winners will be the companies with:
• Qualified production capacity
• Reliable yields
• Real customer contracts
• Actual pricing power

The architecture can win.
Every stock won’t.
$SIVE $AXTI $SOI $LITE $COHR

text:
thinking in bets: Annie something

> Be me and anon
> Keeping it 100% accurate, simple, and anchored to Annie Duke’s "Thinking in Bets"
> Applying the exact book mechanics to this memory photonics / AI trade

> The Setup:
> Sector pulls back hard. Target asset is down 50% from local highs.
> Player 1 (Serenity): Ultra-bullish on AI memory tech, but deeply in the red like most of us even being early 
> Player 2 (Bubble Boy): Perma-bear, sitting in cash, yelling that the memory cycle is dead.

> Step 1: Stop "Resulting" (Chapter 1)
> Book Logic: Never judge the quality of a decision solely by its current outcome. 
> Trade Application: Serenity being down 50% does not mean his fundamental thesis on memory photonics solving the AI bottleneck is wrong (bad timing/luck happens). 

Bubble Boy being in cash doesn't make him a genius if he is blindly ignoring the structural shift in AI hardware. Separate the short-term price action from the long-term tech potential.

> Step 2: Build a Truth-Seeking Pod (Chapter 6)
> Book Logic: Use a diverse group of people to aggressively challenge your biases and spot blind spots.
> Trade Application: Do not join a bullish or bearish echo chamber. Treat Serenity and Bubble Boy as your unwilling pod members. Extract the absolute best-case data from Serenity (tech specs, hyper-scaler demand) and the absolute worst-case data from Bubble Boy (cyclical downturns, oversupply). Find the objective truth in the middle.

> Step 3: Run Mental Time Travel (Chapter 7)
> Book Logic: Map out the future from both success and failure states before making a move.
> Trade Application: 
> Do a Pre-Mortem: Imagine the investment goes to zero. Why did it fail? (e.g., A competitor's tech wins, or hyper-scalers cut budgets). Set your hard exit points based on these specific failure signs *now*.
> Do a Backcasting exercise: Imagine the investment is a massive 10x success. What made it work? (e.g., The memory photonics tech successfully standardizes across AI data centers). Map out how to scale your position to capture this.

> Step 4: Bet in Percentages, Not Certainties (Chapter 2)
> Book Logic: Every choice is a wager. True confidence means saying "I am X% sure," not "I know this will happen."
> Trade Application: Frame your thesis as a probability (e.g., "I am 60% confident this specific memory tech breaks the AI bottleneck"). Allocate a disciplined percentage of your capital that matches that confidence. Do not go all-in like Serenity. Scale in slowly to maintain capital efficiency and protect your downside

What mainstream coverage will miss:
⬩ Filtering photons by quantum statistics isn't a curiosity, it's a core prerequisite for photonic quantum computers and quantum communications alike
⬩ Room-temperature operation removes the single largest hardware cost barrier from that stack
⬩ This compresses the quantum internet timeline, and it does so in photonics specifically, not superconducting, not trapped ion. That distinction matters enormously for who builds the infrastructure layer.

text:
Mid-Month Update: A Valuation Reset, Not a Thesis Reset

The recent selloff has been uncomfortable, but it has also been clarifying.

AI and semiconductor stocks entered July following an extraordinary first-half run. As valuations expanded, expectations left less room for uncertainty. The subsequent correction has been broad and sharp: the semiconductor index has fallen approximately 20% from its June peak, while investors reassess elevated valuations, the economics of AI spending, competition from more efficient Chinese models, and rising geopolitical risk.

This portfolio was never designed to avoid these periods. It is an aggressive, concentrated strategy built around semiconductors, power infrastructure, grid equipment, cooling, and Bitcoin. Drawdowns of 40%–50% are possible over a full cycle, and the strategy should be evaluated over three-to-five-year periods - not several difficult weeks.

The important question is not simply whether prices have declined. It is whether the underlying bottleneck thesis has deteriorated.

So far, the evidence suggests that much of this move is a valuation reset rather than a fundamental thesis reset. TSMC recently reported record quarterly profits, raised its 2026 revenue outlook, and increased planned capital spending in response to continued AI-chip demand. That does not eliminate cyclicality or valuation risk, but it indicates that demand for advanced computing infrastructure remains substantial even as public-market sentiment has weakened.

The portfolio continues to own the scarce inputs that competing AI systems must consume:

* Advanced compute and high-bandwidth memory
* EUV lithography and advanced packaging
* Optical networking and photonics
* Reliable electricity, cooling, and grid expansion
* Bitcoin as a bounded digital-scarcity sleeve

Lower-cost and more efficient AI models deserve careful attention. They could pressure individual software companies and alter the economics of model development. But cheaper inference may also increase overall AI usage. More models, agents, businesses, and users can create more aggregate demand for memory bandwidth, networking, power, and data-center infrastructure - even when the cost of an individual computation falls.

That is why we do not react to every AI headline or automatically buy every decline. We are monitoring hyperscaler capital spending, HBM supply, packaging demand, optical deployment, power-market conditions, earnings guidance, and evidence of actual value capture. A falling stock is not automatically inexpensive, and a strong business can still decline further if its valuation was excessive.

However, valuation resets can create better long-term entry points than periods of widespread enthusiasm. For investors who understand the risks, have a multi-year horizon, and continue to believe in the chokepoint thesis, this may be a sensible environment to dollar-cost average gradually rather than attempting to identify the exact market bottom.

Dollar-cost averaging does not guarantee a profit or protect against further losses. Its advantage is behavioral discipline: capital is deployed incrementally at progressively reset valuations instead of being concentrated into a single prediction about short-term market direction.

Our approach remains unchanged:
Own the bottlenecks, not the apps.

Volatility is expected. Thesis impairment matters more. We will continue adding selectively when prices weaken and the scarcity, demand, and value-capture thesis remains intact - and we will reduce or remove positions if the fundamentals no longer justify ownership.

The selloff may not be over. But for patient investors, periods when conviction is tested are often more attractive for gradual accumulation than periods when confidence is universal and valuations assume perfection.

Disclaimer: AI Bottlenecks + Bitcoin is a model portfolio strategy and is not personalized investment advice, tax advice, legal advice, or a recommendation that any individual investor buy, sell, or hold any security. The strategy is concentrated, thematic, long-only, and high risk. Holdings and weights may change. Bitcoin exposure is held through IBIT and carries significant volatility and regulatory risk. As valuations expanded, expectations left less room for", "id_str": "2078537977819701344", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "2052787352376868866"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzg1Mzc5Nzc1NTEzNDc3MTI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Mid-Month Update: A Valuation Reset, Not a Thesis Reset The recent selloff has been uncomfortable, but it has also been clarifying. AI and semiconductor stocks entered July following an extraordinary first-half run. As valuations expanded, expectations left less room for uncertainty. The subsequent correction has been broad and sharp: the semiconductor index has fallen approximately 20% from its June peak, while investors reassess elevated valuations, the economics of AI spending, competition from more efficient Chinese models, and rising geopolitical risk. This portfolio was never designed to avoid these periods. It is an aggressive, concentrated strategy built around semiconductors, power infrastructure, grid equipment, cooling, and Bitcoin. Drawdowns of 40%–50% are possible over a full cycle, and the strategy should be evaluated over three-to-five-year periods - not several difficult weeks. The important question is not simply whether prices have declined. It is whether the underlying bottleneck thesis has deteriorated. So far, the evidence suggests that much of this move is a valuation reset rather than a fundamental thesis reset. TSMC recently reported record quarterly profits, raised its 2026 revenue outlook, and increased planned capital spending in response to continued AI-chip demand. That does not eliminate cyclicality or valuation risk, but it indicates that demand for advanced computing infrastructure remains substantial even as public-market sentiment has weakened. The portfolio continues to own the scarce inputs that competing AI systems must consume: * Advanced compute and high-bandwidth memory * EUV lithography and advanced packaging * Optical networking and photonics * Reliable electricity, cooling, and grid expansion * Bitcoin as a bounded digital-scarcity sleeve Lower-cost and more efficient AI models deserve careful attention. They could pressure individual software companies and alter the economics of model development. But cheaper inference may also increase overall AI usage. More models, agents, businesses, and users can create more aggregate demand for memory bandwidth, networking, power, and data-center infrastructure - even when the cost of an individual computation falls. That is why we do not react to every AI headline or automatically buy every decline. We are monitoring hyperscaler capital spending, HBM supply, packaging demand, optical deployment, power-market conditions, earnings guidance, and evidence of actual value capture. A falling stock is not automatically inexpensive, and a strong business can still decline further if its valuation was excessive. However, valuation resets can create better long-term entry points than periods of widespread enthusiasm. For investors who understand the risks, have a multi-year horizon, and continue to believe in the chokepoint thesis, this may be a sensible environment to dollar-cost average gradually rather than attempting to identify the exact market bottom. Dollar-cost averaging does not guarantee a profit or protect against further losses. Its advantage is behavioral discipline: capital is deployed incrementally at progressively reset valuations instead of being concentrated into a single prediction about short-term market direction. Our approach remains unchanged: Own the bottlenecks, not the apps. Volatility is expected. Thesis impairment matters more. We will continue adding selectively when prices weaken and the scarcity, demand, and value-capture thesis remains intact - and we will reduce or remove positions if the fundamentals no longer justify ownership. The selloff may not be over. But for patient investors, periods when conviction is tested are often more attractive for gradual accumulation than periods when confidence is universal and valuations assume perfection. Disclaimer: AI Bottlenecks + Bitcoin is a model portfolio strategy and is not personalized investment advice, tax advice, legal advice, or a recommendation that any individual investor buy, sell, or hold any security. The strategy is concentrated, thematic, long-only, and high risk. Holdings and weights may change. Bitcoin exposure is held through IBIT and carries significant volatility and regulatory risk. Dollar-cost averaging does not ensure a profit or prevent losses. text: I love to see startup pitching theoretical discovery for market. Look at theoretical physics: SUSY is a beautiful theory which was proven not to be real by experiments on LHC. Experiments tell you what’s real and what’s not. You can create as many as you like ways to do quantum computing (and I did it myself during my PhD), but only lab will tell you what will be possible. If you really want to win discovery market, automate experiments instead: automate nanofabrication, photonics, chemistry in a physical way. Theory is not enough without experiments.

text:
Let me challenge my own bullish take:

What if photonics is not a bubble but the stock prices were?

AI demand can remain extremely strong while valuations, timelines and revenue expectations still reset much lower.

A great technology does not automatically make every price a great investment.

The next earnings will show who has real execution and who was mostly narrative.

$AAOI $AXTI $SIVE $LITE $COHR

Calling photonics a bubble before CPO has even started scaling is wild.

$AAOI, $AXTI, $SIVE, $LITE and $COHR are being priced like the AI infrastructure cycle is ending…

while the actual optical buildout has barely begun.

Either the entire industry is wrong

or this sell-off

text:
🚨: Scientists Discover a Fundamental Limit to Measuring an Electron in Space and Time ⚛️⏱️

Researchers from the Regensburg Center for Ultrafast Nanoscopy and the Max Planck Institute have identified a new fundamental limit in quantum physics, showing that an electron's exact position and the precise moment it moves cannot both be measured with perfect accuracy.

Published in Nature Photonics, the team used an ultrafast scanning tunneling microscope combined with advanced laser pulses to observe electrons tunneling across atomic-scale gaps. Their measurements revealed an intrinsic response delay of about 500 attoseconds (500 quintillionths of a second) between the arrival of light and the electron's motion.

text:
You said it yourself, it happens in every company

Just like AI is supporting the global economy and will only be useful IF IT ENDS UP EXECUTING

At the end of the day, that's what investing is all about, right?

Believing that a company can execute and justify higher valuations

$SIVE doesn't just rely on its photonics branch

It has many more: SATCOM, Defense, Wireless, LiDAR, etc

It might hit some execution bumps along the way, as always happens

But if even just one of all those branches executes perfectly, it would already justify a much higher share price than the current one

Just one

Now imagine what would happen if several of them execute well, even if it's not to perfection

That's why, among other reasons, I am long on $SIVE

text:
On vit une période très particulière pour les investisseurs en Semi en ce moment ...

Je profite de ce moment pour faire un point sur les différents drawdown de ces dernières années et leurs causes 

Que retrouvons-nous ? 

Sur les 10 dernières années (2016 → mi-2026), $SOXX a connu 3 drawdowns majeurs de plus de 30 % 

Voici le classement clair, du plus important au moins important, avec les dates, l’ampleur et les raisons principales :

1. 2021-2022 : -46,24 % (le plus gros)
•Pic : fin décembre 2021 (~185 $)
•Creux : 14 octobre 2022 (~99,56 $)
•Durée : ~10 mois
•Raisons principales :
oSursaut d’inventaires après le boom COVID (les entreprises avaient trop stocké).
oHausses agressives des taux de la Fed (de 0 % à plus de 5 %) pour combattre l’inflation → compression des valorisations des actions de croissance/tech.
oRotation « growth → value » et craintes de récession.
oCycle semi-conducteurs classique après une période de forte demande.

2. 2024-2025 : -41,67 % (2e plus gros)
•Pic : 10 juillet 2024
•Creux : 8 avril 2025
•Durée : ~9 mois
•Raisons principales :
oPrise de bénéfices après les très fortes hausses liées à l’IA (Nvidia & co.).
oInterrogations sur la soutenabilité des dépenses capex IA des hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta, Amazon) et le délai avant que ça se traduise en revenus réels.
oTensions géopolitiques États-Unis/Chine : restrictions d’exportation de puces IA, tarifs potentiels sous Trump, charges importantes annoncées par Nvidia et AMD sur leurs ventes en Chine.
oSurcapacité dans certains segments + demande plus faible hors IA (PC, smartphones).
oRotation sectorielle temporaire et craintes de valorisations trop élevées après l’euphorie IA.
C’est le drawdown le plus récent avant celui en cours (et presque aussi violent que celui de 2022).

3. 2020 (COVID) : -34,25 %
•Pic : 19 février 2020
•Creux : 20 mars 2020
•Durée : seulement ~1 mois (très rapide)
•Raisons principales :
oCrash boursier mondial lié à la pandémie de Covid-19.
oCraintes de confinement généralisé et d’arrêt de l’économie.
oPerturbations des chaînes d’approvisionnement (même si le secteur s’est très vite redressé grâce à la demande digitale).

4.     2018 (Guerre commerciale) : -26,14 %
oRaisons : guerre commerciale Trump-Chine, tarifs sur les produits tech, craintes de restrictions d’exportation de puces vers la Chine (Huawei, etc.).

Synthèse sur 10 ans
RangPériodeDrawdownDurée approx.Cause principale
12021-2022-46,2 %10 moisHausses taux + surstock
22024-2025-41,7 %9 moisIA + géopolitique Chine
32020-34,3 %1 moisCovid
-2018-26,1 %~9 moisGuerre commerciale

En rajoutant celui de cette année on retrouve cela : 

Drawdowns majeurs de $SOXX sur les ~10-11 dernières années (2015 → 17 juillet 2026)

RangPériodeDrawdownDurée approx.StatutRaisons principales
1Déc. 2021 – Oct. 2022-46,24 %~10 moisTerminéHausses agressives des taux Fed + surstock post-COVID + compression des valorisations tech

2Juil. 2024 – Avr. 2025-41,67 %~9 moisTerminéPrise de bénéfices IA, craintes sur la rentabilité des capex IA, restrictions export Chine (Nvidia/AMD), surcapacité partielle

3Fév. – Mars 2020-34,25 %~1 moisTerminéCrash Covid-19 (confinements mondiaux + peur d’arrêt économique)

4Mars 2018 – Déc. 2018-26,14 %~9 moisTerminéGuerre commerciale Trump-Chine + craintes de restrictions sur les exportations de puces

522 juin – 17 juil. 2026-20,34 %~25 joursEn coursReprise de profit + réévaluation des valorisations IA (attentes trop hautes sur la monétisation des capex), signaux techniques (possible death cross), concurrence chinoise (DeepSeek), et rotation sectorielle

Points clés sur le drawdown d’aujourd’hui :
•C’est le plus important des 12 derniers mois 
•Il est encore modéré par rapport aux 3 plus gros des 10 dernières années.
•Il est très récent et rapide (principalement concentré sur les 3-4 dernières semaines).
•Raisons du moment : les investisseurs commencent à douter que les énormes dépenses en IA se traduisent rapidement en bénéfices concrets, avec en plus des tensions géopolitiques et des prises de bénéfices après la très forte hausse de 2025-2026.

Maintenant, essayons de trouver du positif dans l’Histoire : combien de temps à revenir à l’ATH ? 

Drawdowns majeurs de $SOXX (dernières ~11 années) + temps de récupération

PériodeDrawdownDéclin (pic → creux)Récupération (creux → retour au pic précédent)Temps total (pic → retour au pic)Statut / Date de récupération

Déc. 2021 – Oct. 2022-46,24 %291 jours (~9,5 mois)426 jours (~14 mois)717 jours (~1 an 11 mois)Récupéré le 14 déc. 2023

Juil. 2024 – Avr. 2025-41,67 %272 jours (~9 mois)163 jours (~5,5 mois)435 jours (~14 mois)Récupéré le 18 sept. 2025

Fév. – Mars 2020-34,25 %30 jours (~1 mois)77 jours (~2,5 mois)107 jours (~3,5 mois)Récupéré le 5 juin 2020

2018 (Guerre commerciale)-26,14 %287 jours (9,5 mois)100 jours (~3,3 mois)387 jours (~12,7 mois)Récupéré en avril 2019

22 juin – 17 juil. 2026 (actuel)-20,34 %25 joursEn coursEn coursToujours en drawdown (pas encore revenu au pic de juin 2026)

Interprétation rapide :
•Le plus long à récupérer : celui de 2021-2022 (presque 2 ans pour revenir au pic).
•Le plus rapide : celui de 2020 (Covid) → seulement 2,5 mois de récupération grâce au rebond ultra-rapide.
•Le plus récent terminé (2024-2025) : récupération relativement rapide (~5,5 mois).
•Celui d’aujourd’hui : seulement 25 jours de déclin pour l’instant → beaucoup plus court que les gros drawdowns précédents, mais on ne sait pas encore combien de temps il mettra à se résorber.

Note optimiste sur « revenir à ATH » : 

Dans la plupart des cas, une fois revenu au pic précédent, le titre a rapidement fait un nouveau plus haut historique (sauf pendant les périodes très plates). Le temps indiqué ci-dessus est donc très proche du moment où il a refait un ATH.

En résumé, les drawdown sur les Semi sont relativement fréquents, et le retour à l’ATH , même s’il prend du temps, est systématique sur les 10 dernières années 

Ne perdez pas confiance, ne vendez pas au plus bas, tenez bon 

The darkest hour is just before the dawn

Watching the market finally wake up to the Scandium, Tungsten, Photonics, Liquid Cooling, drone and CPO supercycle is going to feel exactly like this. 😎🚀

The thesis is playing out. Time to get ready.

text:
2026년 4월에 $sive에 Director Global Quality로 입사한 Karan M.이 2시간 전에 올린 글. 

참고로 Karan M.은 $sive에서 Photonics 및 Wireless 비즈니스 전반의 품질 관리를 총괄하고 있음. 근무지는 미국으로 나옴. 

“뛰어난 팀원들, 그리고 고객사와의 미팅을 통해 자율주행 차량과 차세대 AI를 가능하게 할 반도체 기술을 발전시키는 동시에, VDA 6.3을 통해 품질을 한층 더 강화할 수 있었던 스웨덴과 글래스고에서의 보람찬 몇 주였습니다. Sivers Semiconductors 팀의 일원이 될 수 있어 감사할 따름입니다. 이제 시차 적응해야지.”

즉, Karan M.이 이번에 스웨덴과 글래스고를 방문해서 2026년 4분기에 양산이 시작될 LiDAR 칩 대량양산 관련 점검하고 온 것으로 보임.

좋은 소식들 좀 더 나오길.. $sive

text:
I went down a rabbit hole few months ago, and 99% sure who Serenity is. 

Given his professional background, I would follow him on general AI stocks like $nbis, $rddt etc. and niche stocks around optics, photonics, lidar and car electronics any day. He knows his stuff. 

Anything apart from these area - I would do my own research to validate his thesis  

Also, I’m grateful that he has shared a lot of information on X on how he thinks and what is possible in stock market, and growth on X 

Rather than fixating on who he is, you should focus how he thinks, learn as much as possible, so that you can find nuggets like $SIVE and $AXTI in different sectors

text:
Selloffs hitting everything semis, photonics, neoclouds, and SaaS names. $NVDA , $AMD , $MRVL , $AVGO , $AAOI , $NOK , $IREN , and $PLTR all feeling the pressure.

Geopolitical tensions with Iran are pushing oil prices higher, weighing on growth and tech. Housing data’s been weak too listing prices saw their sharpest drops in years this spring.

SpaceX IPO ( $SPCX ) in mid-June lined up right when this rotation picked up. Now you’ve got profit-taking after the big AI run, AI bubble fears creeping in, and money not rotating anywhere obvious not to crypto or gold. Just broad de-risking across the board.

That’s it, there’s nothing fundamentally wrong with these companies just be patient. NFA

text:
AI runs on data, and all of it has to be stored. The problem: most of the world's data still lives on tech patented decades ago.

Folio Photonics built a better way, by borrowing materials science from another industry entirely.

What helped transform the solution into a growing company? Venture capital, and the added support and partnership that came with it.

Check out our latest Startup Stories feature: https://t.co/PiKOqoqybt

$AEHR delivered another powerful confirmation that the AI infrastructure boom continues to expand. This time, confirmation came from the production-test layer of the semiconductor supply chain.

Q4 revenue rose 34% to $18.8 million, adjusted EPS reached $0.11 versus a year-ago loss, gross margin expanded 1,000 basis points to 45%, and bookings surged more than 500% to a record $60.7 million. Effective backlog now stands near $101 million, supporting fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $130-150 million, representing 160%-200% growth, and roughly 66% above consensus at the midpoint.

AI processors generated approximately 71% of annual revenue, silicon photonics contributed another 20%, and together they accounted for more than 80% of Q4 sales.

Aehr's lead AI processor customer doubled its installed systems and shifted all production screening to wafer-level burn-in. A hyperscaler placed a $41 million order supporting high-volume AI-chip production. A separate top-tier accelerator, CPU, and networking supplier completed a benchmark that exceeded expectations and now wants to move a current high-volume device into pilot production. Crucially, guidance largely excludes that new customer, HBM, and high-bandwidth flash, leaving meaningful upside.

That's a "good-as-it-gets" quarter. Stacked up against what $ASML $TSM and Samsung have said in the last two weeks, we're getting confirmation of fundamental strength across the whole AI supply stack. ASML confirms equipment orders and fab expansion, TSMC confirms record advanced-chip production, Samsung confirms booming memory demand, and Aehr confirms those chips and optical interconnects are entering volume manufacturing.

That full-chain acceleration is difficult to reconcile with "peak spending" fears. The remaining question is still hyperscaler spending durability. That's why AI stocks still aren't catching a bid. If $META $AMZN $GOOGL $MSFT etc. reaffirm or raise forward capex, the combined evidence should decisively undermine oversupply fears and confirm that recent semiconductor weakness was a positioning-driven buying opportunity, with no operating downturn underway.

I've shorted the peak of hype stocks successfully multiple times and have gotten 0 credit, yet people that pile in to stocks that everyone else is buying get praised like they are Jesus Christ themselves. 

First I got quantum and nuclear: $IONQ $RGTI $QBTS $OKLO

Then I got space: $ASTS (and $PLTR )

Then I got photonics and semis: $LITE $AAOI $SMH

Doing what did is 100x more difficult.  I don't know why I don't get credit but I am trying to figure it out.  Maybe it's because other's can't replicate it.  Or maybe people don't like shorting.  Maybe I'm shadow banned.

Idk

text:
TL;DR

• India approved a $13.3 billion semiconductor package to accelerate domestic chip manufacturing.

• Intel became the first company to mass-produce chips using ASML's High NA EUV lithography.

• The U.S. launched a new AI cybersecurity initiative connecting frontier AI firms with critical infrastructure.

• Nokia and NVIDIA unveiled the world's first commercial AI-Native RAN for next-generation mobile networks.

• Tower Semiconductor announced a $3 billion Japan expansion to boost AI chip and silicon photonics production.

• Australia proposed a new AI governance framework focused on copyright and responsible AI.

• Anthropic launched Claude for Teachers, expanding AI into education with student privacy protections.

• Aehr Test Systems posted record AI-driven semiconductor orders and raised its long-term outlook.

• The U.S. NTIA opened applications for AI-native wireless network funding to advance next-generation telecom.

• OpenAI pushed back against Apple's trade secret lawsuit, escalating a closely watched legal battle.

• New York advanced a moratorium on new hyperscale data centers while reviewing AI infrastructure impacts.

text:
DAILY TECH NEWS ROUNDUP 🚨

Everything important that happened in tech during the last 24 hours:

• India approved a fresh ₹1.28 trillion ($13.3 billion) semiconductor package to expand its domestic chip manufacturing strategy. The new investment significantly scales India's long-term effort to become a global semiconductor production hub and reduce dependence on imports.

• ASML announced Intel Foundry has begun high-volume manufacturing of select Panther Lake Core Ultra Series 3 processors using ASML's EXE High NA EUV lithography on the Intel 18A process. Intel becomes the first company to ship high-volume logic chips built with High NA EUV, marking a major milestone for next-generation semiconductor manufacturing and enabling denser, higher-performance AI chips.

• The White House announced a new AI and cybersecurity coordination group that will connect frontier AI companies with operators of critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to help identify and respond to software vulnerabilities discovered by advanced AI systems before they can be exploited by attackers.

• Nokia launched the world's first commercial AI-Native RAN platform in partnership with NVIDIA. The platform integrates AI directly into mobile radio infrastructure and already delivers more than 20% spectral efficiency gains, with pilots beginning later this year and broader rollout planned for 2027.

• Tower Semiconductor announced a $3 billion expansion of its 300mm silicon photonics, silicon germanium, and advanced packaging capacity in Japan, backed by $1 billion in government funding. The investment targets surging demand for AI data center optical connectivity and advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

• Australia unveiled a new national AI policy framework focused on copyright protections for creators and responsible AI deployment. The proposal signals continued regulatory momentum around generative AI governance and now heads to national cabinet.

• Anthropic introduced Claude for Teachers, expanding Claude into education with a commitment not to train its models on student conversations. The launch strengthens competition for AI adoption across schools and universities.

• Aehr Test Systems reported record quarterly orders of $60.7 million and a backlog exceeding $100 million, driven by AI semiconductor, silicon photonics, and power device testing demand. The company also issued strong FY2027 revenue guidance, highlighting continued investment in AI chip infrastructure.

• The U.S. NTIA opened applications for a new AI-Native Wireless Network Innovation funding program aimed at accelerating AI-powered wireless infrastructure. The initiative is designed to support next-generation networking technologies and strengthen the domestic telecom ecosystem.

• Reports emerged that OpenAI is pushing back against Apple's trade secret lawsuit, marking the latest escalation in one of the industry's most closely watched legal battles. The dispute could influence future relationships between major AI developers and platform owners.

• New York's proposed moratorium on new hyperscale data centers continued drawing attention as policymakers evaluate the electricity and water demands of AI infrastructure. The proposal could influence future data center permitting policies in other U.S. states.

text:
Huge breakthrough in practical quantum technologies 👀

'Scientists just engineered a room temperature quantum material called a Quantum Statistical Plasmonic Metacrystal (QSPM) that can distinguish, route and preserve different quantum states of light without requiring cryogenic cooling.'

'Unlike most quantum materials that require temperatures near absolute zero, the artificial quantum material works at room temperature, overcoming one of the biggest barriers in quantum photonics.'

'Researchers believe it could accelerate the development of more scalable quantum computers, quantum communication networks, quantum sensors and integrated photonic chips.'

Today marks an exciting new chapter for PsiQuantum’s team and mission as we drive the future of computing.​

Victor Peng has been named Chief Executive Officer following his interim appointment in February.​

PsiQuantum is also welcoming Rob Soderbery as Executive Vice President and Sriram Sitaraman as Chief Information Officer.​

Together with our entire workforce, these leaders will help scale our company's engineering, technology, and operational capabilities as we build the world’s first utility-scale quantum computers and further advance our state-of-the-art silicon photonics platform. Learn more: https://t.co/Rm97sjZ7n5

They are not the same thing. In fact, understanding the difference is the key to spotting where the actual hardware value is being created. Here is the ultimate cheat sheet on how the tech works, and how AmpliTech Group ($AMPG) owns the physical layer of both: 🧱 1. Open RAN: The "Lego" Network -The Concept: Historically, telecom networks were closed, proprietary monopolies. If a carrier bought Ericsson hardware, they had to use Ericsson software. Open RAN (Open Radio Access Network) shatters this lock-in by standardizing interfaces. Now, carriers can mix and match software and hardware from different vendors. -$AMPG’s Role: They are the ultimate US hardware champion. $AMPG designed and commercialized the O-RAN CAT B 64T64R Massive MIMO radio unit. -The Validation: At the Spring 2026 O-RAN ALLIANCE Global PlugFest, $AMPG was the only vendor with a radio of this highly advanced configuration, proving seamless multi-vendor interoperability alongside giants like AT&T and Deutsche Telekom. 🧠 2. AI-RAN: The "Shared-Brain" Network -The Concept: While Open RAN is about modular hardware and software, AI-RAN is about embedding AI processing directly into the network’s cellular architecture. Instead of running cell towers on single-purpose proprietary silicon, AI-RAN runs both cellular operations (RAN) and AI workloads (like computer vision or edge computing) on the same shared, GPU-accelerated servers. It essentially turns every tower base into an edge-AI data center. -$AMPG’s Role: AI-RAN requires extremely high-end, zero-distortion hardware to process massive amounts of data in real-time. $AMPG’s radio was selected as the core hardware platform for the world’s first open-source Massive MIMO AI-RAN demonstration, developed in collaboration with NVIDIA and Northeastern University. -The Validation: Their 64T64R radio was the only Massive MIMO radio featured in live demonstrations for AI-RAN Alliance members at VIAVI’s VALOR Lab—the industry’s first AI-RAN Alliance-endorsed testing facility. 🚀 The Bottom Line No matter which standard carriers prioritize, they cannot build next-gen networks without physical, high-performance, US-manufactured radio frequency hardware. If they want open, interoperable systems, they need $AMPG's certified 64T64R radio unit. If they want to deploy neural-network intelligence at the edge, they rely on the physical RF and amplifier hardware trusted by NVIDIA and the AI-RAN Alliance. $AMPG is uniquely positioned right at the bottleneck of both multi-billion-dollar revolutions. 📈📡 #AMPG #OpenRAN #AIRAN #NVIDIA #5G #Telecom #ValueInvesting #TechHardware"}}}, "rest_id": "2077401096264302877", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1273", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2077460781050826776", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "99", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Interesting divergence in the Test & Packaging layer today.

The photonics-specific names ran:
$AEHR +26% (record bookings, SiPh burn-in orders, FY27 guide ~2x consensus)
ShunSin +10%
Ibiden +7.6%
Santec +4%

The diversified ATE/OSAT names didn't:
$TER -3.4%
$AMKR -3.2%
$VIAV -3.4%

One day, not a trend; but today, the market paid for photonics test exposure specifically, not test exposure generally.

text:
NTT just put $500M behind photonics and almost nobody connected it to $POET.

The IOWN AI Fund launched June 10. NTT, SK Group, $CHT, the Development Bank of Japan, roughly $ 500M. The stated investment targets include photonics technologies and light sources and modulators, lasers, VCSELs, the whole layer. $AVGO, $GFS, $GLW, Samsung and SK hynix are all supporting it.

Now remember who POET is already working with. NTT Innovative Devices, NTTs own device arm, co-developing a 100G bidirectional optical engine for mobile front haul. Prototypes this year, high volume production 2027. Suresh called it out at the AGM as a brand new vertical they didnt have access to a year ago.

To be clear the fund invests in startups, its not funding POET and I'm not saying it is. But its NTT telling you exactly where they think the next decade of infrastructure goes, and POET already has a co-development seat inside the NTT family on that same bet.

Everyone models POET as an AI datacenter play. Mobile front haul is a completely separate market with completely separate customers, and NTT is one of the largest carriers on earth building the standard the rest of Asia may end up adopting. Thats optionality nobody has in their model right now.
